what_it_does: Acceptance test for cc_clone that checks the reflection-persistence bug fix and clone:// addressing: it writes a clone record to a temp clones directory, mocks the supervisor message response, calls onboard_clone, and asserts that the returned reflection is persisted on the durable clone record, that a provenance-stable clone://<source_sid>/<at> address is derived and stored, and that get_by_address resolves the clone plus reflection and fails loudly on unknown addresses.
observations: Uses a custom PASS/FAIL harness instead of unittest/pytest
Sets a temporary CLONES_DIR and writes a clone JSON record by hand
Mocks cc_clone.msg_clone with a lambda returning a fixed reflection
Tests both the happy path and an unknown-address failure path
Cleans up the temp directory before exiting
Docstring explicitly instructs a falsify-first run against unmodified code
standouts: The central assertion that reflection must be persisted on disk
Provenance-stable clone:// address derived from source_sid and at
get_by_address point lookup returning clone plus reflection
conventions: Docstring states purpose, run command, and expected failure mode
Relative sys.path insertion to import runtime.cc_clone
Exit code 1 when any test fails
concerns: Monkey-patches cc_clone.CLONES_DIR and cc_clone.msg_clone globally without restoration
Hard-coded UUIDs and paths in the test record
No formal test framework; custom harness may be harder to integrate
Cleans up temp dir only after all assertions, so an early exception could leave files
notes: A lightweight, self-contained acceptance test that drives cc_clone through mocked supervisor I/O to verify reflection durability and stable clone addressing.
questions: What is the exact expected schema of a clone record after the fix?
Does onboard_clone always derive and write the address, or only in the reflect phase?
What exception type and message does get_by_address raise for unknown addresses?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it tests reflection persistence and clone:// addressing, including a falsify-first assertion.
intention_signals: The file is trying to encode the desired contract for cc_clone: a supervised clone's reflection must survive beyond the live message exchange by being written to the durable record, and clones must be addressable by a stable URI that lets consumers look them up later.
novelty: The clone://<source_sid>/<at> provenance-stable address and the get_by_address consumer lookup, combined with a falsify-first acceptance test for reflection persistence.
contribution: Provides an executable acceptance contract that defines the expected durable clone record fields (reflection, address) and the behavior of clone:// address derivation and lookup for cc_clone.
summary_for_embedding: An acceptance test that verifies a clone's reflection is persisted on its durable record and retrievable via a stable clone://<source_sid>/<at> address.
intention_for_embedding: To falsify-first and then guard the fix for reflection persistence and clone URI addressing in cc_clone.

symbols.does: Custom lightweight assertion helper that records pass/fail status and prints each result.
symbols.description: The ok function appends the test name to either PASS or FAIL based on the boolean cond, prints an 'ok' or 'FAIL' line, and the script later exits with code 1 if FAIL is non-empty. It drives the entire acceptance test harness in this file.
